title: Witch Week - Chapter 12 (Fragment 3)
text:
“Perhaps,” Chrestomanci’s voice said out of the air, “you could all bring yourselves to hold hands? I have no idea where the Old Gate House is, you see.”

Thankfully, everyone grabbed for everyone else. Nan found herself holding Brian’s hand and Charles Morgan’s. She had never thought the time would come when she would be glad to do that. Estelle had managed to be the one holding Chrestomanci’s hand. That became clear when the line of them began to move briskly down to the path that led into town and Estelle’s voice could be heard in front, piping up in answer to Chrestomanci’s questions.

As soon as there was no chance of anyone else hearing them, Chrestomanci began asking a great many questions. He asked who was prime minister, and which were the most important countries, and what was the EEC, and how many world wars there had been. Then he asked about things from history. Before long, everyone was giving him answers, and feeling a little superior, because it was really remarkable the number of things Chrestomanci seemed not to know. He had heard of Hitler, though he asked Brian to refresh his memory about him, but he had only the haziest notion about Gandhi or Einstein, and he had never heard of Walt Disney or reggae. Nor had he heard of Dulcinea Wilkes. Nan explained about Dulcinea, and found herself saying, with great pride, that she was descended from Dulcinea.

Why am I saying that? she thought, in sudden alarm. I don’t really know it’s safe to tell him! And yet, as soon as she thought that, Nan began to see why she had said it. It was the way Chrestomanci was asking those questions. It reminded Nan of the time she had kept coming out in a rash, and her aunt had taken her to a very important specialist. The specialist had worn a very good suit, though it was nothing like as beautiful as Chrestomanci’s, and he had asked questions in just the same way, trying to get at Nan’s symptoms. Remembering this specialist made Nan feel a lot more hopeful. If you thought of Chrestomanci, in spite of his vagueness and his elegance, as a sort of specialist trying to solve their problems, then you could believe that he might just be able to help them. He was certainly a strong and expert witch. Perhaps he could make the old lady send them somewhere really safe.

When the path led them into the busy streets of the town, Chrestomanci stopped asking questions, but it was clear to Nan that he went on finding symptoms. He made everyone stop while he examined a truck parked by the supermarket. It was just an ordinary truck with Leyland on the front of it and Heinz Meanz Beanz on the side, but Chrestomanci murmured “Good Lord!” as if he was really astonished, before dragging them over to look through the windows of the supermarket. Then he towed them up and down in front of some cars. This part was really frightening. The car windows, the hubcaps, and the glass of the supermarket all showed faint, misty reflections of the six of them. They were quite sure some of the people who were shopping would notice any second.

At last, Chrestomanci let Estelle drag him up the street as far as the tatty draper’s where nobody ever seemed to buy anything. “How long have you had decimal currency?” he asked. While they were telling him, the misty reflection in the draper’s window showed his tall shape bending to look at some packets of tights and a dingy blue nylon nightdress. “What are these stockings made of?”

vocabulary.definition: to respond to a question or request
vocabulary.example: That became clear when the line of them began to move briskly down to the path that led into town and Estelle’s voice could be heard in front, piping up in answer to Chrestomanci’s questions.

vocabulary.definition: very surprised
vocabulary.example: ” as if he was really astonished, before dragging them over to look through the windows of the supermarket.

vocabulary.definition: in an active, quick, or energetic way
vocabulary.example: That became clear when the line of them began to move briskly down to the path that led into town and Estelle’s voice could be heard in front, piping up in answer to Chrestomanci’s questions.

vocabulary.definition: a currency system based on units of ten
vocabulary.example: “How long have you had decimal currency?

vocabulary.definition: to come from an ancestor or source
vocabulary.example: Nan explained about Dulcinea, and found herself saying, with great pride, that she was descended from Dulcinea.

vocabulary.definition: to pull something or someone with force
vocabulary.example: ” as if he was really astonished, before dragging them over to look through the windows of the supermarket.

vocabulary.definition: graceful and stylish appearance or manner
vocabulary.example: If you thought of Chrestomanci, in spite of his vagueness and his elegance, as a sort of specialist trying to solve their problems, then you could believe that he might just be able to help them.

vocabulary.definition: to inspect or investigate carefully
vocabulary.example: He made everyone stop while he examined a truck parked by the supermarket.

vocabulary.definition: causing fear or alarm
vocabulary.example: This part was really frightening.

vocabulary.definition: most unclear or uncertain
vocabulary.example: He had heard of Hitler, though he asked Brian to refresh his memory about him, but he had only the haziest notion about Gandhi or Einstein, and he had never heard of Walt Disney or reggae.

vocabulary.definition: of great significance or value
vocabulary.example: He asked who was prime minister, and which were the most important countries, and what was the EEC, and how many world wars there had been.

vocabulary.definition: to say something quietly or softly
vocabulary.example: It was just an ordinary truck with Leyland on the front of it and Heinz Meanz Beanz on the side, but Chrestomanci murmured “Good Lord!

vocabulary.definition: a conception or belief about something
vocabulary.example: He had heard of Hitler, though he asked Brian to refresh his memory about him, but he had only the haziest notion about Gandhi or Einstein, and he had never heard of Walt Disney or reggae.

vocabulary.definition: to suddenly start speaking or making a sound
vocabulary.example: That became clear when the line of them began to move briskly down to the path that led into town and Estelle’s voice could be heard in front, piping up in answer to Chrestomanci’s questions.

vocabulary.definition: the head of a government in some countries
vocabulary.example: He asked who was prime minister, and which were the most important countries, and what was the EEC, and how many world wars there had been.

vocabulary.definition: images seen in a mirror or shiny surface
vocabulary.example: The car windows, the hubcaps, and the glass of the supermarket all showed faint, misty reflections of the six of them.

vocabulary.definition: worthy of attention; striking
vocabulary.example: Before long, everyone was giving him answers, and feeling a little superior, because it was really remarkable the number of things Chrestomanci seemed not to know.

vocabulary.definition: protected from or not exposed to danger or risk
vocabulary.example: I don’t really know it’s safe to tell him!

vocabulary.definition: a person who is an expert in a particular area or field
vocabulary.example: It reminded Nan of the time she had kept coming out in a rash, and her aunt had taken her to a very important specialist.

vocabulary.definition: a large self-service store selling foods and household goods
vocabulary.example: He made everyone stop while he examined a truck parked by the supermarket.

vocabulary.definition: signs or indications of a condition or illness
vocabulary.example: The specialist had worn a very good suit, though it was nothing like as beautiful as Chrestomanci’s, and he had asked questions in just the same way, trying to get at Nan’s symptoms.

vocabulary.definition: lack of clarity or distinctness
vocabulary.example: If you thought of Chrestomanci, in spite of his vagueness and his elegance, as a sort of specialist trying to solve their problems, then you could believe that he might just be able to help them.
